title: Liquids for use with electro-wetting on dielectric active optical switch
abstract: An active optical switch system that has a substrate having at least a first waveguide and a waveguide-fluid coupling area and a fluid channel above the substrate. A first liquid, having a first refractive index, is within the fluid channel. A second liquid, immiscible with the first liquid and having a second refractive index, is also within the fluid channel. The second refractive index is different from the first refractive index. At least one of the first and second liquids is selectively movable relative to the waveguide-fluid coupling area so as to affect an effective refractive index of the first waveguide. The optical switch system also includes a fluidic driving mechanism for moving the liquids within the fluid channel. The fluidic driving mechanism has at least one electrode positioned to apply an electric field to at least the first fluid in the fluid channel.
claims: Claims Claims What we claim as the invention is: 1. An active optical switch system, comprising: a substrate having at least a first waveguide and a waveguide-fluid coupling area; a fluid channel above the substrate; a first liquid within the fluid channel, the first liquid having a first refractive index; a second liquid within the fluid channel, the second liquid being immiscible with the first liquid and having a second refractive index, the second refractive index being different from the first refractive index by at least 0.18, the first and second liquids being selectively movable relative to the waveguide-fluid coupling area so as to affect an effective refractive index of the first waveguide; and a fluidic driving mechanism for moving the first and second liquids within the fluid channel, the fluidic driving mechanism comprising at least one electrode positioned to apply an electric field to at least the first fluid in the fluid channel; wherein the second liquid has a dynamic viscosity less than 50 cP, the value of dynamic viscosity being determined at 25 Â°C, the first liquid is a polar liquid having a relative permittivity, Îµ Î , greater than 20, and the first and second liquids each have a boiling point higher than 70 Â°C. 2. A system as recited in claim 1, wherein the first and second liquids each have a melting point no higher than -40 Â°C. 3. A system as recited in claim 1, wherein the second liquid has a dynamic viscosity less than 30 cP, where the value of dynamic viscosity is determined at 25 Â°C. 4. A system as recited in claim 3, wherein the second liquid has a dynamic viscosity less than 20 cP, where the value of dynamic viscosity is determined at 25 Â°C. 5. A system as recited in claim 1, wherein the first liquid has a relative permittivity, Îµ Î , greater than 40. 6. A system as recited in claim 1, wherein the first liquid has a relative permittivity, 8 r , greater than 80. 7. A system as recited in claim 1, wherein the one of the first and second liquids has a density no more than 20% different from the density of the other of the first and second liquids. 8. A system as recited in claim 1, wherein the one of the first and second liquids has a density no more than 10% different from the density of the other of the first and second liquids. 9. A system as recited in claim 1, wherein the first and second liquids each have an optical absorbance over the range 1250 nm - 1550 nm of less than 3 dB/cm. 10. A system as recited in claim 9, wherein the first and second liquids each have an optical absorbance over the range 1250 nm - 1650 nm of less than 3 dB/cm. 11. A system as recited in claim 1, wherein the first liquid is selected from hydroxy propylene carbonate, ethylene glycol, and admixtures thereof with water. 12. A system as recited in claim 1, wherein the second liquid is selected from diphenyl sulfide and triphenyl sulfide. 13. A system as recited in claim 1, wherein at least one of the first and second liquids is at least partially deuterated. 14. A system as recited in claim 1, wherein the first liquid is selected from hydroxy propylene carbonate, a 1 : 1 mixture of hydroxy propylene carbonate and water, and ethylene glycol, the second liquid is selected from diphenyl sulfide and triphenyl sulfide. 15. A system as recited in claim 14, wherein at least one of the first and second liquids is at least partially deuterated. 16. A system as recited in claim 15, wherein both the first and second liquids are at least partially deuterated. 17. A system as recited in claim 1, wherein one of the first and second liquids has a room temperature density no more than 45% different from the room temperature density of the other of the first and second liquids.
